summary: Matched the common fighter HammerKneeBend action file, replacing 332 bytes of assembly in asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s with C implementations for ftCo_800C5B88 and the HammerKneeBend Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks. The PR also moved the relevant declarations into a new ftCo_HammerKneeBend.h header, added the file to obj_files.mk, introduced a hammerkneebend motion-vars struct in ftCommon/types.h for fp+2340/fp+2344/fp+2348, and made small formatting/tooling cleanup changes driven by clang-format/vim settings. No review comments were present in the slice.
what_changed: Deleted the HammerKneeBend assembly block from asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s, including ftCo_800C5B88, ftCo_HammerKneeBend_Anim, ftCo_HammerKneeBend_IASA, ftCo_HammerKneeBend_Phys, ftCo_HammerKneeBend_Coll, and the local sdata2 constants ftCo_804D8D90/ftCo_804D8D94.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_HammerKneeBend.c with a 100% matching 332-byte implementation according to the PR report.

Implemented ftCo_800C5B88 as the HammerKneeBend state entry/setup: stores arg1 at fp->mv.co.hammerkneebend.x4, clears x0, initializes x8 from fp->co_attrs.jump_startup_time, computes flags with ftCo_800C54C4, changes motion to ftCo_MS_HammerKneeBend, and calls ftCo_800C4E94.
Implemented ftCo_HammerKneeBend_Anim to call ftCo_800C4F64, transition via ftCo_800C5A98 when the hammerkneebend timer is <= 0, then decrement fp->mv.co.hammerkneebend.x8.
Implemented thin forwarding callbacks: IASA calls ftCo_800CB59C, Phys calls ft_80084F3C, and Coll calls ftCo_HammerWait_Coll.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_HammerKneeBend.h with address-annotated prototypes and a GALE01_0C5B88 include guard.
Removed HammerKneeBend prototypes from the broader src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.h header and included the new HammerKneeBend header from ftCo_HammerJump.c and ftCo_Init.c.
Added ftCo_HammerKneeBend.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk so the new C translation unit replaces the removed assembly portion in the build.
Added a hammerkneebend struct to union ftCommon_MotionVars mapping fp+2340 to x0, fp+2344 to x4, and fp+2348 to float x8.

Changed .clang-format AllowShortIfStatementsOnASingleLine from false to Never and adjusted several existing files for spacing/label formatting, such as block labels changing from `block_9 :` to `block_9:` and operators such as `&0x7f` to `& 0x7f`.
Updated tools/vimrc project autocmds: renamed the augroup to Melee, split C/doxygen filetype handling, set C doxygen syntax on FileType c, and enabled g:c_syntax_for_h.
smart_moves: The matched C mirrors the assembly’s motion-variable offsets through a dedicated ftCommon_MotionVars sub-struct, making fp+2340/fp+2344/fp+2348 semantics searchable and reusable instead of leaving them as raw offsets.
The state callbacks were kept minimal and direct, preserving the original assembly shape for the small 32-byte wrapper functions by delegating to existing shared helpers.

The entry function preserves match-sensitive ordering by declaring `flags` before `Fighter* fp`; the inline comment notes that moving it below fp breaks matching and may indicate an inline-related codegen constraint.
The PR isolated the newly matched action into ftCo_HammerKneeBend.c/.h and removed those symbols from ft_0C31.h, improving header locality for future ftCommon action decompilation.
The MUST_MATCH-only unused stack array documents and gates an otherwise artificial codegen requirement, keeping normal builds cleaner while preserving exact matching behavior where needed.

Adding the object file immediately before the remaining ft_0C31.s object in obj_files.mk allowed a partial extraction from a larger assembly file without requiring the whole ft_0C31 block to be matched.
decomp_lessons: For partial decompilation of large fighter assembly files, add the new C object immediately before the residual assembly object and delete only the matched symbols from the asm file.

When a fighter action uses fp motion-var offsets, encode those offsets in the appropriate ftCommon_MotionVars union member as soon as the action is matched; this makes later action code much easier to read and prevents repeated raw-offset reasoning.
Tiny callback functions often match as direct wrappers around existing helpers; resist overcomplicating Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll implementations when the assembly shows a single call and return.
Variable declaration order can be match-critical. In this PR, `u32 flags` had to remain before `Fighter* fp`, with the author noting that moving it below fp suggests an inline/codegen issue.
MUST_MATCH blocks can be used to preserve artifacts such as unused stack allocation while documenting that they are only present for matching.

Newly matched action files should get their own local header and include it where action tables or neighboring action files need prototypes, rather than accumulating prototypes in a generic numbered header.

Formatting/tooling changes can appear alongside decomp matches; the evidence here indicates these were mostly mechanical style cleanups caused by clang-format/vim configuration rather than behavior changes.
naming_conventions: Common fighter action files follow the ftCo_<ActionName>.c and ftCo_<ActionName>.h pattern, shown here by ftCo_HammerKneeBend.c/h.
Action callbacks use the suffix convention `_Anim`, `_IASA`, `_Phys`, and `_Coll`, as in ftCo_HammerKneeBend_Anim, ftCo_HammerKneeBend_IASA, ftCo_HammerKneeBend_Phys, and ftCo_HammerKneeBend_Coll.
Unidentified but address-known functions retain ftCo_800Cxxxx names with address comments in headers, such as `/* 0C5B88 */ void ftCo_800C5B88(...)`.
New include guards in this slice use GALE01 address-style names, such as GALE01_0C5B88 for ftCo_HammerKneeBend.h and GALE01_0679B0 for fighter.h.
Motion variable fields for not-yet-semantically-named data use offset-like placeholders x0, x4, and x8 inside an action-specific struct, here `mv.co.hammerkneebend`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced the assembly range beginning at 800C5B88 through 800C5CD0 with C and left the next assembly symbol ftCo_800C5CD4 in ft_0C31.s.
Used a MUST_MATCH-only `u8 _[8] = { 0 };` stack artifact in ftCo_800C5B88 to account for unused stack requirements visible in the original assembly.
Preserved declaration/order sensitivity by keeping `u32 flags;` before `Fighter* fp = GET_FIGHTER(gobj);`; the source comment says moving it below fp prevents matching.
Modeled sdata2 constants from assembly as literal arguments in C where possible: the change-motion call uses `1` and `0`/`NULL`, corresponding to former ftCo_804D8D90 and ftCo_804D8D94 uses.
Kept fp->mv.co.hammerkneebend.x8 as a float timer initialized from jump_startup_time and decremented with `--`, matching the assembly subtract-by-1.0 behavior.
Used shared existing helpers to match single-call wrapper functions: ftCo_800CB59C for IASA, ft_80084F3C for physics, and ftCo_HammerWait_Coll for collision.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale was available; conclusions are based on the title, matching report, changed files, and diff excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that renamed or semantically refined ftCo_800C5B88, ftCo_800C54C4, ftCo_800C548C, ftCo_800C4E94, ftCo_800C4F64, or ftCo_800C5A98.
Look for subsequent work on ftCo_HammerJump, ftCo_HammerWait, ftCo_HammerFall, ftCo_HammerWalk, and ftCo_HammerTurn to see whether hammer action motion vars were unified or renamed.
Search for other uses of MUST_MATCH unused stack arrays to identify common CodeWarrior stack-allocation matching patterns.
Check whether the hammerkneebend x0/x4/x8 motion-var fields later received semantic names.
Investigate whether the formatting/tooling changes in .clang-format and tools/vimrc caused or enabled similar label/operator formatting cleanups in adjacent PRs.
